ab: inabayan! papilrang ku kanmu muka kirepanauwa dra maaidrangan i drekalr an uninan.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: aiwa! tarapuyuma mi kimadrayar kana maaidrangan.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說南王卑南族語。
en: B: Okay! We’re going to speak Southern King Puyuma to the seniors.

ab: adalrep ta lra meredek?
en: A: Are we almost there?

ab: adalrep lra.
en: B:Almost there.

ab: mumu! druwa mi kirepanauwa kanu!
en: A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: iuwa! saigu mu temarapuyuma i! salaw inaba i!
ch: B:哇!你們會說南王卑南族語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B: Wow! You guys speak Southern King Puyuma! That’s awesome!

ab: inabayan! an uninan i, papilrang ku kanmu muka kikaruna i dawadawa.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.

ab: sinsi, aiwa!
en: B: Okay, teacher.

ab: alamu kadrini uturik. adri kababalu kemasu dra sinadaday.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: sinsi, aiwa! padangaw lra naniyam sinadaday.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.

ab: meredek ta lra?
en: A: Are we there yet?

ab: adri driya! adawilr driya!
en: B: Not yet! It’s far away.

ab: uwa puenay!
en: A: You go watering.

ab: aiwa! puenaya ku!
en: B: Okay, I’ll water it.

ab: uwa alak kana trapetrapan yuyu.
en: A: You go get the bird catcher.

ab: aiwa! benawia ku dra ayam.
en: B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: inabayan! an uninan i, auka ta dratu kinadruwan kana garamegaman.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ab: iuwa dra inaba i! kuwadakuda ta muka?
ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?

ab: muisatra ta dra bas.
en: A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: uturik kadri peniya! tremimaa ta dra ritus.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.

ab: aiwa!
en: B: Yes.

ab: kadri nguwayan i, a ayaayam. kadri likudran i, a unaunan. karananetayan menau.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.

ab: sinsi, inedang ku dra unan.
en: B: I’m afraid of snakes.

ab: an kemadrini i, uwa yuyu nau dra ayam.
ch: A:如果是這樣，那你去看鳥。
en: A: If that’s the case, then you go and see the birds.

ab: an saya waluwan i, karebeliyas maradruka ta.
ch: A:我們一個小時後回來集合。
en: A: We’ll be back in an hour.

ab: aiwa! sinsi, semangalan!
en: B: Yes, thank you.

ab: menau yu dra manay kandri na wari?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: menau ku dra sadru dra unan aw dra sadru dra ayam.
ch: B:我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.

ab: kamelamu! uwaburek ta lra!
en: A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: ngarayu ku driya! rapi ku dapalr.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.

ab: marenanga ta muka melranguya maw?
en: A: Shall we go swimming together?

ab: aiwa! alra!
en: B:Yes! Let’s go!

ab: sadru a trau na melralranguy kadrini.
en: A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: asabak na enay kadriyu. melranguya ta kadrini.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
